channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hamas frees 6 hostages but questions cloud Gaza ceasefire's future
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas on Saturday released the last six living hostages expected under the first phase of its ceasefire with Israel with a week remaining, as growing questions over the next phase clouded the fragile deal 's future.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: USDA scholarship for students at historically Black colleges suspended
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal scholarship aimed at boosting students from underserved and rural areas attending historically Black colleges and universities has been put on hold. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Assocaited Press, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Sepsis a threat in pope's pneumonia battle as Vatican Holy Year celebrations march on without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Vatican carried on with its Holy Year celebrations without the pope Saturday, as Pope Francis battled pneumonia and a complex respiratory infection that doctors say remains touch-and-go and will keep him hospitalized for at least another week.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: Witnesses and authorities described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver’s license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Lovan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump moves with light speed and brute force in shaking the core of what America has been
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is moving with light speed and brute force to break the existing order and reshape America at home and abroad. He likes the ring of calling himself king. No one can absorb it all.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US offers UN resolution on war in Ukraine that's far short of rival European statement
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States has proposed a draft U.N. resolution that stops far short of a competing European-backed statement demanding an immediate withdrawal of all of Moscow’s forces from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Quiet Florida neighborhood rocked after three found dead, child kidnapped
item.description: Authorities are investigating a triple homicide involving a Florida man who was also the subject of an Amber Alert for his young daughter last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow, begging to go back inside
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, heard a child nearby begging to go back inside. When he saw the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas, he called 911.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, NewsNation

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former federal worker shares frustration on TikTok after losing job in less than 2 months
item.description: A federal worker who lost her job after being in her position for less than two months hopped on TikTok to tell her story as well as share information and resources to help other federal workers in the same position.
item.dc:creator: Ya-Marie Sesay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: A federal judge has adjourned the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court on how to handle the Justice Department's request to drop charges against the Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgia woman says she'll 'never fully recover' after IVF mix-up leads to her losing custody of baby
item.description: A Georgia's woman's intimate journey with IVF left her feeling "violated" after she gave birth to someone else's child, a lawsuit claims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inmates takeover another New York prison, all visits canceled day after Gov. Hochul deploys National Guard
item.description: Dozens of correctional facilities canceled prison visits one day after New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order to provide National Guard members to provide extra security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Fox 5

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, ABC 4

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Unions sue over Trump admin efforts to fire probationary federal employees
item.description: A coalition of government employee unions sued late Wednesday over the Trump administration’s efforts to fire employees still in their probationary period across the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Trump backs idea to send 20% of DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
